scenarios.title: An abandoned finding survives a round trip through the store
scenarios.description: Finding F-093 — the stage-adjustment collapse — is the demo's payload. It must be writable with its reason intact and readable back with the reason attached, because the reason IS the value.
preconditions: The graph store is migrated to the Breadcrumbs-v2 vocabulary
steps: Write F-093 with status 'abandoned' and a reason explaining the effect collapsed after stage adjustment
expected: The finding is returned with status 'abandoned' and its reason text intact
The reason is not truncated or dropped

scenarios.title: An abandoned finding without a reason is rejected
scenarios.description: The human gate. An abandoned attempt with no stated reason is worthless to the next researcher, so the store refuses it rather than accepting a hollow row.
steps: Attempt to write a finding with status 'abandoned' and no reason field
expected: The write is rejected with an error naming the finding

scenarios.title: Migrating the committed database preserves every row and edge
scenarios.description: ingestion/breadcrumbs.db is committed and shared by the team. The CHECK-constraint rebuild drops and recreates the findings table, and finding_edges cascades on delete — so a careless migration silently destroys the graph. Run it twice; nothing may change the second time.
preconditions: A copy of the committed ingestion/breadcrumbs.db with at least one session, finding, and edge
steps: Record row counts for chat_messages, findings, and finding_edges
Run the migration a second time
Re-read the row counts and the live DDL from sqlite_master
expected: Row counts are unchanged after both runs — no edge was cascade-deleted
The findings CHECK constraint now admits 'open' and the edge CHECK admits 'duplicate_of'
The second run is a no-op

scenarios.title: A conversation correction becomes an auditable patch only after approval
scenarios.description: A model proposes a scoped constraint from a K Pro exchange, but the graph remains unchanged until a researcher approves the exact source-linked Memory Diff.
preconditions: The referenced K Pro message is ingested
steps: Submit the candidate without approved_by and observe rejection
Approve the same candidate with an exact evidence quote
Read the stored knowledge item back
expected: No row exists after the unapproved attempt
The approved row preserves its quote, source, aliases, typed conditions, samples, calculated metrics, and action delta
The derived FTS5 row is created only for the approved patch

scenarios.title: Provenance creates canonical provisional people and contribution edges
scenarios.description: Existing author and reviewer provenance becomes an expertise graph without guessing that different spellings belong to the same human.
preconditions: Reviewed findings or approved knowledge exist
steps: Connect to the store and backfill people/contributions
Write another item whose author differs only by case and whitespace
expected: Case and whitespace variants share one stable provisional person ID
Authorship and review remain distinct roles
Reconnecting does not duplicate contribution edges
